Phase-48.NAME: VPN Config Download MIME and Filename Hardening
Phase-48.purpose: Harden VPN configuration downloads so Android, iOS, and desktop browsers save WireGuard and AmneziaVPN configs as the original .conf files instead of appending .txt.

scope.in: Backend download response MIME and headers, safe .conf filename policy, frontend Blob MIME alignment for config downloads, compatibility tests, static smoke, and MyGRACE metadata.
scope.out: Deploy/install scripts, nginx/docker-compose, TLS/certificate handling, VPN profile generation, AWG/WG cryptographic material, QR rendering, raw config content changes, and mobile app-specific import automation.

Phase-48.problem: Mobile browsers can rename vpn.conf to vpn.conf.txt when the server returns config downloads as text/plain; the current KrotPN download surface must make the file an attachment with non-text MIME and explicit .conf filename metadata.

steps.step-1: Inventory current config download surfaces and confirm /api/v1/vpn/config/download is the canonical server-side attachment endpoint.
steps.step-2: Add a small backend response helper that UTF-8 encodes config text and returns application/octet-stream without a text/plain charset.
steps.step-3: Add safe Content-Disposition with both filename="krotpn-*.conf" and filename*=UTF-8''krotpn-*.conf, plus X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff and Cache-Control: no-store, private.
steps.step-4: Implement filename sanitation that removes separators, unsafe characters, duplicate extensions, and any accidental .txt suffix while enforcing exactly one .conf suffix.
steps.step-5: Update frontend config download Blob creation to use application/octet-stream for both backend-returned blobs and locally available managed-device config text.
steps.step-6: Add or extend backend compatibility tests for headers, body preservation, authentication, and no regression to pending-trial first config access.
steps.step-7: Add Phase-48 static smoke that fails on text/plain download responses, missing filename-star/nosniff headers, .conf.txt filenames, or deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose changes.
steps.step-8: Run backend router tests, frontend build, Phase-48 smoke, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, and deploy/install diff guard.

data-flow.flow-1: User taps download in mobile cabinet -> frontend requests /api/v1/vpn/config/download -> backend returns bytes with application/octet-stream and attachment .conf filename.
data-flow.flow-2: Managed config already loaded in frontend -> frontend creates Blob with application/octet-stream -> browser download attribute stays krotpn-*.conf.
data-flow.flow-3: QR/copy/raw config paths continue to use existing config text and are not transformed by the attachment-specific MIME hardening.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Android Chrome receives Content-Type application/octet-stream and Content-Disposition attachment with filename krotpn-user.conf, so it stores krotpn-user.conf instead of krotpn-user.conf.txt.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: iOS Safari receives the same attachment and explicit filename metadata; even if it previews through Files, the suggested filename remains .conf.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: A verified pending-trial user still reaches the first config path from Phase-45, but the response wrapper changes from plain text to a safe attachment.

phase-gates.gate-1: Download endpoint does not use text/plain for .conf attachment responses.
phase-gates.gate-2: Content-Disposition includes attachment, filename, and filename-star values that end in .conf and never .txt.
phase-gates.gate-3: Frontend-created Blobs specify application/octet-stream and preserve .conf download names.
phase-gates.gate-4: Config body content, QR display, copy action, and pending-trial first config eligibility remain compatible.

risks.risk-1: Some mobile browsers may still present a preview UI, but attachment plus octet-stream plus explicit filename is the strongest server-side control available.
risks.risk-2: Changing config content or VPN profile rendering would invalidate clients; Phase-48 only changes response metadata and frontend Blob type.
risks.risk-3: Non-ASCII filenames can behave inconsistently across mobile download managers, so Phase-48 keeps generated filenames ASCII-safe.

commands.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_vpn_router_compat.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-48.xml docs/modules/M-066.xml docs/verification/V-M-066.xml docs/graph-index.xml docs/plan-index.xml docs/verification-index.xml
